prompt:
Fix NixOS module YAML generation: telegram config fields are not nested under the chat key.

## Problem
In nix/module.nix line 160-163, the `lib.optionalString` for telegram config inserts a blank line that breaks YAML nesting. The generated config has `telegram_chat_id` and `telegram_allowed_users` at the top level instead of under `chat:`.

## Root cause
Line 160-163:
```nix
backend: "${cfg.chatBackend}"${lib.optionalString (cfg.chatBackend == "telegram") ''

      telegram_chat_id: ${toString cfg.chatTelegramChatID}
      telegram_allowed_users: [${lib.concatMapStringsSep ", " toString cfg.chatTelegramAllowedUsers}]''}
```

The `''\n` after the opening of optionalString creates a blank line in the YAML output. YAML treats blank lines as breaking the current mapping context, so the telegram fields end up as top-level keys.

## Fix
Remove the blank line so the telegram fields stay indented under chat:

```nix
backend: "${cfg.chatBackend}"${lib.optionalString (cfg.chatBackend == "telegram") ''
        telegram_chat_id: ${toString cfg.chatTelegramChatID}
        telegram_allowed_users: [${lib.concatMapStringsSep ", " toString cfg.chatTelegramAllowedUsers}]''}
```

Make sure the indentation is correct — the telegram fields need the same indent level as `backend:` (8 spaces, under `chat:`).

## File to modify
- nix/module.nix (lines 160-163)

## Testing
- Build the module and inspect the generated config: verify telegram_chat_id and telegram_allowed_users are under chat:
- Also verify the non-telegram path still works (no extra blank lines when backend is googlechat)
- Run: go test ./...

## Acceptance criteria
- Generated YAML has telegram fields properly nested under chat:
- No blank line between backend and telegram_chat_id
- googlechat backend config still generates correctly
